sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Fund a 9-month continuation of Scalus covering maintenance, Dijkstra hard-fork readiness, interoperability across JVM and JS/TS, and a scoped application runtime.

Deliverables: Ongoing maintenance including bug fixes, dependency upkeep, security patches, and releases, Dijkstra developer preview with initial Plutus V4 support in the compiler and standard library, Updates for Dijkstra ledger rules, transaction building, cost models, nested transactions, accounts, guard scripts, and conformance testing, Improved JS/TS interfaces and documentation for components embedded in MeshJS, Evolution SDK, and Lucid Evolution, Java/Kotlin integration examples and cleaner integration paths with Cardano Client Lib and Yaci, Foundational runtime components including reactive workers, a chain follower, and task scheduler
Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Scalus is an established open-source Cardano development platform built by Lantr Engineering over three years of continuous delivery.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal asks for ₳2,464,844 over 9 months, approximately $394,375 at a $0.16/ADA reference rate, with no contingency.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The current proposal is a reduced resubmission from a prior ₳8.503M, 12-month proposal, with FTE reduced from 8.25 to 2.25 and the standalone L1 node, full L2 integration, and broad formal verification removed from scope.
Claim (adoption, independently_verifiable, high materiality): Scalus components are reused in Cardano Client Lib, YaciDevKit, MeshJS SDK, Evolution SDK, and Lucid Evolution.
Claim (adoption, independently_verifiable, high materiality): Protocols and applications identified as built on Scalus include Gummiworm L2, Bifrost, SugarRush, Vela, and DID/DIDComm.
Claim (adoption, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): The proposal says Scalus has 4,642 commits across 32 releases, 12 contributors, and Scalus.js has more than 20,000 downloads per month.
missing: Independent evidence for: Scalus is an established open-source Cardano development platform built by Lantr Engineering over three years of continuous delivery.

Independent evidence for: Dijkstra readiness is presented as necessary because Dijkstra introduces Plutus V4 support, nested transactions, guard script types, and accounts affecting smart contract development, evaluation, transaction building, ledger rules, testing, and workflows.

Independent evidence for: Delivery is described as milestone-based and administered through audited SundaeSwap treasury contracts with an independent oversight board, third-party assurance, public reporting, and open community sessions.
Independent evidence for: The proposal frames Scalus as shared Apache 2.0 open-source infrastructure intended to reduce repeated assembly work across many Cardano teams, tools, and products.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal asks for ₳2,464,844 over 9 months, approximately $394,375 at a $0.16/ADA reference rate, with no contingency." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Scalus is an established open-source Cardano development platform built by Lantr Engineering over three years of continuous delivery." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
